verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc simile erit regnum cælorum decem virginibus: quæ accipientes lampades suas exierunt obviam sponso et sponsæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ὁμοιωθήσεται ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν δέκα παρθένοις αἵτινες λαβοῦσαι τὰς λαμπάδας ἀυτῶν ἐξῆλθον εἰς ἀπάντησιν τοῦ νυμφίου
verses.king_james: Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins, which took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegroom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quinque autem ex eis erant fatuæ, et quinque prudentes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πέντε δὲ ἦσαν ἐξ αὐτῶν φρόνιμοι καὶ αἱ πέντε μωραὶ
verses.king_james: And five of them were wise, and five *were* foolish.

verses.latin_vulgate: sed quinque fatuæ, acceptis lampadibus, non sumpserunt oleum secum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αἵτινες μωραὶ λαβοῦσαι τὰς λαμπάδας ἑαυτῶν οὐκ ἔλαβον μεθ᾽ ἑαυτῶν ἔλαιον
verses.king_james: They that *were* foolish took their lamps, and took no oil with them:

verses.latin_vulgate: prudentes vero acceperunt oleum in vasis suis cum lampadibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αἱ δὲ φρόνιμοι ἔλαβον ἔλαιον ἐν τοῖς ἀγγείοις αὐτῶν μετὰ τῶν λαμπάδων ἀυτῶν
verses.king_james: But the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.

verses.latin_vulgate: Moram autem faciente sponso, dormitaverunt omnes et dormierunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χρονίζοντος δὲ τοῦ νυμφίου ἐνύσταξαν πᾶσαι καὶ ἐκάθευδον
verses.king_james: While the bridegroom tarried, they all slumbered and slept.

verses.latin_vulgate: Media autem nocte clamor factus est: Ecce sponsus venit, exite obviam ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μέσης δὲ νυκτὸς κραυγὴ γέγονεν ἰδού ὁ νυμφίος ἔρχεται ἐξέρχεσθε εἰς ἀπάντησιν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And at midnight there was a cry made, Behold, the bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc surrexerunt omnes virgines illæ, et ornaverunt lampades suas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἠγέρθησαν πᾶσαι αἱ παρθένοι ἐκεῖναι καὶ ἐκόσμησαν τὰς λαμπάδας ἀυτῶν
verses.king_james: Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fatuæ autem sapientibus dixerunt: Date nobis de oleo vestro, quia lampades nostræ extinguuntur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αἱ δὲ μωραὶ ταῖς φρονίμοις εἶπον δότε ἡμῖν ἐκ τοῦ ἐλαίου ὑμῶν ὅτι αἱ λαμπάδες ἡμῶν σβέννυνται
verses.king_james: And the foolish said unto the wise, Give us of your oil; for our lamps are gone out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt prudentes, dicentes: Ne forte non sufficiat nobis, et vobis, ite potius ad vendentes, et emite vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθησαν δὲ αἱ φρόνιμοι λέγουσαι μήποτε οὐκ ἀρκέσῃ ἡμῖν καὶ ὑμῖν πορεύεσθε δὲ μᾶλλον πρὸς τοὺς πωλοῦντας καὶ ἀγοράσατε ἑαυταῖς
verses.king_james: But the wise answered, saying, *Not so*; lest there be not enough for us and you: but go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dum autem irent emere, venit sponsus: et quæ paratæ erant, intraverunt cum eo ad nuptias, et clausa est janua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπερχομένων δὲ αὐτῶν ἀγοράσαι ἦλθεν ὁ νυμφίος καὶ αἱ ἕτοιμοι εἰσῆλθον μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ εἰς τοὺς γάμους καὶ ἐκλείσθη ἡ θύρα
verses.king_james: And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came; and they that were ready went in with him to the marriage: and the door was shut.

verses.latin_vulgate: Novissime vero veniunt et reliquæ virgines, dicentes: Domine, domine, aperi nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὕστερον δὲ ἔρχονται καὶ αἱ λοιπαὶ παρθένοι λέγουσαι κύριε κύριε ἄνοιξον ἡμῖν
verses.king_james: Afterward came also the other virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille respondens, ait: Amen dico vobis, nescio vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν οὐκ οἶδα ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: But he answered and said, Verily I say unto you, I know you not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vigilate itaque, quia nescitis diem, neque horam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γρηγορεῖτε οὖν ὅτι οὐκ οἴδατε τὴν ἡμέραν οὐδὲ τὴν ὥραν ἐν ᾗ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἔρχεται
verses.king_james: Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the Son of man cometh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut enim homo peregre proficiscens, vocavit servos suos, et tradidit illis bona sua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὦσπερ γὰρ ἄνθρωπος ἀποδημῶν ἐκάλεσεν τοὺς ἰδίους δούλους καὶ παρέδωκεν αὐτοῖς τὰ ὑπάρχοντα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For the kingdom of heaven is as a man travelling into a far country, *who* called his own servants, and delivered unto them his goods.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et uni dedit quinque talenta, alii autem duo, alii vero unum, unicuique secundum propriam virtutem: et profectus est statim.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ᾧ μὲν ἔδωκεν πέντε τάλαντα ᾧ δὲ δύο ᾧ δὲ ἕν ἑκάστῳ κατὰ τὴν ἰδίαν δύναμιν καὶ ἀπεδήμησεν εὐθέως
verses.king_james: And unto one he gave five talents, to another two, and to another one; to every man according to his several ability; and straightway took his journey.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abiit autem qui quinque talenta acceperat, et operatus est in eis, et lucratus est alia quinque.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πορευθεὶς δέ ὁ τὰ πέντε τάλαντα λαβὼν εἰργάσατο ἐν αὐτοῖς καὶ ἐποίησεν ἄλλα πέντε τάλαντα
verses.king_james: Then he that had received the five talents went and traded with the same, and made *them* other five talents.

verses.latin_vulgate: Similiter et qui duo acceperat, lucratus est alia duo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡσαύτως καὶ ὁ τὰ δύο ἐκέρδησεν καὶ αὐτὸς ἄλλα δύο
verses.king_james: And likewise he that *had received* two, he also gained other two.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem unum acceperat, abiens fodit in terram, et abscondit pecuniam domini sui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ τὸ ἓν λαβὼν ἀπελθὼν ὤρυξεν ἓν τῇ γῇ καὶ ἀπέκρυψεν τὸ ἀργύριον τοῦ κυρίου αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: But he that had received one went and digged in the earth, and hid his lord's money.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post multum vero temporis venit dominus servorum illorum, et posuit rationem cum eis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ χρόνον πολὺν ἔρχεται ὁ κύριος τῶν δούλων ἐκείνων καὶ συναίρει μετ᾽ αὐτῶν λόγον
verses.king_james: After a long time the lord of those servants cometh, and reckoneth with them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et accedens qui quinque talenta acceperat, obtulit alia quinque talenta, dicens: Domine, quinque talenta tradidisti mihi, ecce alia quinque superlucratus sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσελθὼν ὁ τὰ πέντε τάλαντα λαβὼν προσήνεγκεν ἄλλα πέντε τάλαντα λέγων κύριε πέντε τάλαντά μοι παρέδωκας ἴδε ἄλλα πέντε τάλαντα ἐκέρδησα ἐπ᾽ αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And so he that had received five talents came and brought other five talents, saying, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents: behold, I have gained beside them five talents more.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illi dominus ejus: Euge serve bone, et fidelis: quia super pauca fuisti fidelis, super multa te constituam; intra in gaudium domini tui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔφη δέ αὐτῷ ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ εὖ δοῦλε ἀγαθὲ καὶ πιστέ ἐπὶ ὀλίγα ἦς πιστός ἐπὶ πολλῶν σε καταστήσω εἴσελθε εἰς τὴν χαρὰν τοῦ κυρίου σου
verses.king_james: His lord said unto him, Well done, *thou* good and faithful servant: thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accessit autem et qui duo talenta acceperat, et ait: Domine, duo talenta tradidisti mihi, ecce alia duo lucratus sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσελθὼν δὲ καὶ ὁ τὰ δύο τάλαντα λαβών εἰπεν κύριε δύο τάλαντά μοι παρέδωκας ἴδε ἄλλα δύο τάλαντα ἐκέρδησα ἐπ᾽ αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: He also that had received two talents came and said, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me two talents: behold, I have gained two other talents beside them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illi dominus ejus: Euge serve bone, et fidelis: quia super pauca fuisti fidelis, super multa te constituam; intra in gaudium domini tui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔφη αὐτῷ ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ εὖ δοῦλε ἀγαθὲ καὶ πιστέ ἐπὶ ὀλίγα ἦς πιστός ἐπὶ πολλῶν σε καταστήσω εἴσελθε εἰς τὴν χαρὰν τοῦ κυρίου σου
verses.king_james: His lord said unto him, Well done, good and faithful servant; thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accedens autem et qui unum talentum acceperat, ait: Domine, scio quia homo durus es; metis ubi non seminasti, et congregas ubi non sparsisti:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσελθὼν δὲ καὶ ὁ τὸ ἓν τάλαντον εἰληφὼς εἶπεν κύριε ἔγνων σε ὅτι σκληρὸς εἶ ἄνθρωπος θερίζων ὅπου οὐκ ἔσπειρας καὶ συνάγων ὅθεν οὐ διεσκόρπισας
verses.king_james: Then he which had received the one talent came and said, Lord, I knew thee that thou art an hard man, reaping where thou hast not sown, and gathering where thou hast not strawed:

verses.latin_vulgate: et timens abii, et abscondi talentum tuum in terra: ecce habes quod tuum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ φοβηθεὶς ἀπελθὼν ἔκρυψα τὸ τάλαντόν σου ἐν τῇ γῇ ἴδε ἔχεις τὸ σόν
verses.king_james: And I was afraid, and went and hid thy talent in the earth: lo, *there* thou hast *that is* thine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem dominus ejus, dixit ei: Serve male, et piger, sciebas quia meto ubi non semino, et congrego ubi non sparsi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ εἶπεν αὐτῷ πονηρὲ δοῦλε καὶ ὀκνηρέ ᾔδεις ὅτι θερίζω ὅπου οὐκ ἔσπειρα καὶ συνάγω ὅθεν οὐ διεσκόρπισα
verses.king_james: His lord answered and said unto him, *Thou* wicked and slothful servant, thou knewest that I reap where I sowed not, and gather where I have not strawed:

verses.latin_vulgate: oportuit ergo te committere pecuniam meam numulariis, et veniens ego recepissem utique quod meum est cum usura.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔδει οὖν σε βαλεῖν τὸ ἀργύριον μου τοῖς τραπεζίταις καὶ ἐλθὼν ἐγὼ ἐκομισάμην ἂν τὸ ἐμὸν σὺν τόκῳ
verses.king_james: Thou oughtest therefore to have put my money to the exchangers, and *then* at my coming I should have received mine own with usury.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tollite itaque ab eo talentum, et date ei qui habet decem talenta:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄρατε οὖν ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ τὸ τάλαντον καὶ δότε τῷ ἔχοντι τὰ δέκα τάλαντα
verses.king_james: Take therefore the talent from him, and give *it* unto him which hath ten talents.

verses.latin_vulgate: omni enim habenti dabitur, et abundabit: ei autem qui non habet, et quod videtur habere, auferetur ab eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῷ γὰρ ἔχοντι παντὶ δοθήσεται καὶ περισσευθήσεται ἀπὸ δὲ τοῦ μὴ ἔχοντος καὶ ὃ ἔχει ἀρθήσεται ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For unto every one that hath shall be given, and he shall have abundance: but from him that hath not shall be taken away even that which he hath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et inutilem servum ejicite in tenebras exteriores: illic erit fletus, et stridor dentium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τὸν ἀχρεῖον δοῦλον ἐκβάλλετε εἰς τὸ σκότος τὸ ἐξώτερον ἐκεῖ ἔσται ὁ κλαυθμὸς καὶ ὁ βρυγμὸς τῶν ὀδόντων
verses.king_james: And cast ye the unprofitable servant into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem venerit Filius hominis in majestate sua, et omnes angeli cum eo, tunc sedebit super sedem majestatis suæ:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ ἔλθῃ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐν τῇ δόξῃ αὐτοῦ καὶ πάντες οἱ ἅγιοι ἄγγελοι μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ τότε καθίσει ἐπὶ θρόνου δόξης αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: When the Son of man shall come in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then shall he sit upon the throne of his glory:

verses.latin_vulgate: et congregabuntur ante eum omnes gentes, et separabit eos ab invicem, sicut pastor segregat oves ab hædis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ συναχθήσεται ἔμπροσθεν αὐτοῦ πάντα τὰ ἔθνη καὶ ἀφοριεῖ αὐτοὺς ἀπ᾽ ἀλλήλων ὥσπερ ὁ ποιμὴν ἀφορίζει τὰ πρόβατα ἀπὸ τῶν ἐρίφων
verses.king_james: And before him shall be gathered all nations: and he shall separate them one from another, as a shepherd divideth *his* sheep from the goats:

verses.latin_vulgate: et statuet oves quidem a dextris suis, hædos autem a sinistris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ στήσει τὰ μὲν πρόβατα ἐκ δεξιῶν αὐτοῦ τὰ δὲ ἐρίφια ἐξ εὐωνύμων
verses.king_james: And he shall set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on the left.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc dicet rex his qui a dextris ejus erunt: Venite benedicti Patris mei, possidete paratum vobis regnum a constitutione mundi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἐρεῖ ὁ βασιλεὺς τοῖς ἐκ δεξιῶν αὐτοῦ δεῦτε οἱ εὐλογημένοι τοῦ πατρός μου κληρονομήσατε τὴν ἡτοιμασμένην ὑμῖν βασιλείαν ἀπὸ καταβολῆς κόσμου
verses.king_james: Then shall the King say unto them on his right hand, Come, ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world:

verses.latin_vulgate: esurivi enim, et dedistis mihi manducare: sitivi, et dedistis mihi bibere: hospes eram, et collegistis me:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπείνασα γὰρ καὶ ἐδώκατέ μοι φαγεῖν ἐδίψησα καὶ ἐποτίσατέ με ξένος ἤμην καὶ συνηγάγετέ με
verses.king_james: For I was an hungred, and ye gave me meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me drink: I was a stranger, and ye took me in:

verses.latin_vulgate: nudus, et cooperuistis me: infirmus, et visitastis me: in carcere eram, et venistis ad me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γυμνὸς καὶ περιεβάλετέ με ἠσθένησα καὶ ἐπεσκέψασθέ με ἐν φυλακῇ ἤμην καὶ ἤλθετε πρός με
verses.king_james: Naked, and ye clothed me: I was sick, and ye visited me: I was in prison, and ye came unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc respondebunt ei justi, dicentes: Domine, quando te vidimus esurientem, et pavimus te: sitientem, et dedimus tibi potum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἀποκριθήσονται αὐτῷ οἱ δίκαιοι λέγοντες κύριε πότε σε εἴδομεν πεινῶντα καὶ ἐθρέψαμεν ἢ διψῶντα καὶ ἐποτίσαμεν
verses.king_james: Then shall the righteous answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, and fed *thee*? or thirsty, and gave *thee* drink?

verses.latin_vulgate: quando autem te vidimus hospitem, et collegimus te: aut nudum, et cooperuimus te?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πότε δέ σε εἴδομεν ξένον καὶ συνηγάγομεν ἢ γυμνὸν καὶ περιεβάλομεν
verses.king_james: When saw we thee a stranger, and took *thee* in? or naked, and clothed *thee*?

verses.latin_vulgate: aut quando te vidimus infirmum, aut in carcere, et venimus ad te?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πότε δέ σε εἴδομεν ἀσθενῆ ἢ ἐν φυλακῇ καὶ ἤλθομεν πρός σε
verses.king_james: Or when saw we thee sick, or in prison, and came unto thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens rex, dicet illis: Amen dico vobis, quamdiu fecistis uni ex his fratribus meis minimis, mihi fecistis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ βασιλεὺς ἐρεῖ αὐτοῖς ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ἐφ᾽ ὅσον ἐποιήσατε ἑνὶ τούτων τῶν ἀδελφῶν μου τῶν ἐλαχίστων ἐμοὶ ἐποιήσατε
verses.king_james: And the King shall answer and say unto them, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye have done *it* unto one of the least of these my brethren, ye have done *it* unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc dicet et his qui a sinistris erunt: Discedite a me maledicti in ignem æternum, qui paratus est diabolo, et angelis ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἐρεῖ καὶ τοῖς ἐξ εὐωνύμων πορεύεσθε ἀπ᾽ ἐμοῦ οἱ κατηραμένοι εἰς τὸ πῦρ τὸ αἰώνιον τὸ ἡτοιμασμένον τῷ διαβόλῳ καὶ τοῖς ἀγγέλοις αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels:

verses.latin_vulgate: esurivi enim, et non dedistis mihi manducare: sitivi, et non desistis mihi potum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπείνασα γὰρ καὶ οὐκ ἐδώκατέ μοι φαγεῖν ἐδίψησα καὶ οὐκ ἐποτίσατέ με
verses.king_james: For I was an hungred, and ye gave me no meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me no drink:

verses.latin_vulgate: hospes eram, et non collegistis me: nudus, et non cooperuistis me: infirmus, et in carcere, et non visitastis me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ξένος ἤμην καὶ οὐ συνηγάγετέ με γυμνὸς καὶ οὐ περιεβάλετέ με ἀσθενὴς καὶ ἐν φυλακῇ καὶ οὐκ ἐπεσκέψασθέ με
verses.king_james: I was a stranger, and ye took me not in: naked, and ye clothed me not: sick, and in prison, and ye visited me not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc respondebunt ei et ipsi, dicentes: Domine, quando te vidimus esurientem, aut sitientem, aut hospitem, aut nudum, aut infirmum, aut in carcere, et non ministravimus tibi?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἀποκριθήσονται αὐτῷ καὶ αὐτοὶ λέγοντες κύριε πότε σε εἴδομεν πεινῶντα ἢ διψῶντα ἢ ξένον ἢ γυμνὸν ἢ ἀσθενῆ ἢ ἐν φυλακῇ καὶ οὐ διηκονήσαμέν σοι
verses.king_james: Then shall they also answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, or athirst, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and did not minister unto thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc respondebit illis, dicens: Amen dico vobis: Quamdiu non fecistis uni de minoribus his, nec mihi fecistis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἀποκριθήσεται αὐτοῖς λέγων ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ἐφ᾽ ὅσον οὐκ ἐποιήσατε ἑνὶ τούτων τῶν ἐλαχίστων οὐδὲ ἐμοὶ ἐποιήσατε
verses.king_james: Then shall he answer them, saying, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye did *it* not to one of the least of these, ye did *it* not to me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ibunt hi in supplicium æternum: justi autem in vitam æternam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπελεύσονται οὗτοι εἰς κόλασιν αἰώνιον οἱ δὲ δίκαιοι εἰς ζωὴν αἰώνιον
verses.king_james: And these shall go away into everlasting punishment: but the righteous into life eternal.
